title: coelorachis rugosa
intro: Coelorachis rugosa is a plant species native to tropical Africa, commonly known as the 'Fringed Club Moss'. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of certain compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Coelorachis rugosa include its potential to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Coelorachis rugosa can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
